title: pi-context pi-bound is a CLI process mode routed before op resolution, not a substrate op
context: pi-bound launches an interactive pi subprocess; it is not a request->response substrate operation. It must not enter the reflected surface:use op set the CLI exposes, and it cannot run through the op-dispatch/auth path.
decision: Expose pi-bound as a bare subcommand (per the cli-command-form convention: an alternate run-and-exit mode is a bare verb, not a flag). Route it inside the CLI main() before op resolution — main() dispatches when the first positional is 'pi-bound' — and exclude it from the reflected op surface. It is a process-launch mode of the pi-context bin, parallel to list-tools' process surface, not a registered op.
consequences: pi-bound bypasses op dispatch and the op auth-gate; its contract is the CLI entry's, not an op's.
The reflected surface:use enumeration is unchanged by adding pi-bound.
Tests target the CLI entry mode (main() dispatch on the bare pi-bound verb), not an op definition.

references.label: pi-bound spec — routing before op resolution
CLI reflects surface:use ops; process surface partitioned out
command-form convention cli-command-form — bare verb for an alternate run-and-exit mode
